claim: C2PA / Content Credentials had over 6,000 members and affiliates by January 2026, with Adobe writing credentials across all Creative Cloud products and Firefly automatically.
quote: over 6,000 members and affiliates have joined the C2PA initiative

claim: Google Pixel 10 (Sept 2025) signs every photo by default with hardware-backed Titan M2 keys; Sony PXW-Z300 is the first camcorder with native C2PA signing; Nikon Z6 III's C2PA firmware was suspended after a critical signing-key vulnerability and certificates were revoked.
quote: Nikon Z6 III added C2PA via firmware in August 2025, then was suspended after a critical signing vulnerability with all certificates revoked

claim: OpenAI joined the C2PA Steering Committee and attaches Content Credentials to images created and edited by DALL-E 3 in ChatGPT and the OpenAI API.
EU AI Act Article 50 requires deployers of AI systems generating deepfakes to disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated, clearly and at the time of first exposure.
quote: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated

claim: Article 50 transparency obligations come into force on 2 August 2026 with exemptions for law enforcement and clearly artistic, satirical or fictional works; breaches risk fines up to EUR 15 million or 3% of global turnover.
quote: The transparency obligations in Article 50 of the Act come into force on 2 August 2026

claim: On 17 December 2025 the European Commission published the first draft Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content to give providers and deployers practical guidance on Article 50 compliance.
quote: Code of Practice on marking and labelling of AI-generated content

claim: The TAKE IT DOWN Act, signed by President Trump on 19 May 2025, criminalises non-consensual intimate imagery including AI-generated deepfakes and forces platforms to remove flagged content within 48 hours; covered platforms have until 19 May 2026 to stand up the notice-and-removal process.
quote: passed the full House on a 409–2 vote on April 28, 2025... signed into law by President Donald Trump on May 19, 2025

claim: Tennessee's ELVIS Act (signed 21 March 2024) was the first US law to explicitly protect a person's voice (including simulations) from AI cloning and targets both deepfake creators and the providers of tools used to make them; violation is a Class A misdemeanour.
quote: first enacted legislation in the United States specifically designed to protect musicians from the unauthorized use of their voices

claim: California AB 1836 amends the state's right of publicity law to prohibit unauthorized digital replicas of deceased performers in expressive audiovisual works or sound recordings.
quote: California state lawmakers recently passed AB 1836, which would modify the state's right of publicity statute to prohibit the use of digital replicas of a deceased person

claim: Peer-reviewed evaluation shows leading deepfake detectors degrade sharply on cross-dataset and unseen generators — XCeption fell from 89.2% to 85.7% on FaceForensics++ without fine-tuning; MDD's AUC dropped from 0.998 to 0.674 cross-dataset.
quote: XCeption exhibiting the most resilience (accuracy decreased from 89.2% to 85.7%)... MDD declines from 0.998 to 0.674 AUC

claim: A 2025 arXiv study documents systematic 10-15% cross-dataset performance degradation and notes detectors trained on GAN-era data fail badly on diffusion-model outputs, meaning detection vendors are perpetually behind generation.
quote: Deepfake Detection that Generalizes Across Benchmarks

claim: The FBI's IC3 issued a May 2025 PSA warning of an ongoing AI-voice and SMS-based deepfake campaign impersonating senior US officials and recommending out-of-band verification.
quote: Senior US Officials Impersonated in Malicious Messaging Campaign

claim: Norton in July 2025 added on-device audio and visual deepfake protection to its 360 mobile apps via Genie AI, letting users submit clips or YouTube links for authenticity scoring.
quote: Norton Adds Audio and Visual Deepfake Protection on Mobile

claim: Post-Arup industry guidance hardened around mandatory out-of-band callbacks, predetermined authentication codes, and dual-officer counter-signature for any high-value transfer instruction received over video or voice.
quote: verification measures that operate outside the communication channel—such as out-of-band callbacks, predetermined authentication codes or mandatory secondary approval

claim: A 2025 prompt-injection flaw in Gmail's Gemini 'Summarize this email' feature let attackers smuggle invisible white-on-white instructions that produced fake Google security alerts in the AI summary; Gmail still relies on its red 'Don't reply or click links' banner as the primary user-facing warning.
quote: Google Gemini flaw hijacks email summaries for phishing

claim: The Brennan Center frames the 'liar's dividend' — politicians and bad actors dismissing real footage as deepfake — as a growing democratic harm, citing Hany Farid and arguing provenance infrastructure and rapid authentication are the practical counter.
quote: Deepfakes, Elections, and Shrinking the Liar's Dividend

claim: TikTok in 2026 auto-labels AI-generated visuals/audio using C2PA Content Credentials plus invisible watermarks and detection models, has labelled over 1.3 billion AI videos, and demotes or removes unlabelled synthetic media; AI-written captions and hashtags are exempt.
quote: labeled over 1.3 billion AI-generated videos using a combination of Content Credentials, invisible watermarking, and detection models

claim: Meta labels AI-generated content with an 'AI info' tag (replacing the prior 'Made with AI' label in July 2024), driven by self-declaration and metadata signals from partner generators.
